title: Executive Order 8065—Establishing the Necedah Migratory Waterfowl Refuge

flags.justification: Grants the Secretary of Agriculture rule-making authority over permitted activities within the refuge, a standard and appropriately-scoped regulatory grant for a conservation area.
A clear, specific designation with an explicit statutory basis.
Multiple related elements (land reservation, criminal prohibition, land-order amendment) all coherently serve one purpose.

The criminal prohibition is reasonably specific ('hunt, trap, capture, wilfully disturb, or kill any bird or wild animal... or enter thereon'); the deferral of specific permitted activities to the Secretary's future regulations is a standard, appropriately-scoped pattern for refuge management.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
A permanent wildlife refuge and conservation designation, not a temporary-authority grant requiring review.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
Standard federal wildlife-refuge criminal enforcement applies; no distinct external-monitoring gap is identifiable.

A larger-scale (approximately 40,500 acres) but routine application of an already well-established conservation mechanism (Migratory Bird Conservation Act refuges with criminal trespass and take prohibitions).
This carefully amends a prior land-classification withdrawal order (EO 6964) to exclude the refuge area, a deliberate, coordinated adjustment.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/16 = 0.0%. Notable among the wildlife-refuge orders in this batch for its larger scale (approximately 40,500 acres) and explicit criminal-enforcement provision, yet it still scores cleanly given its routine, well-established regulatory structure.
